code: @Manual{,   title = {addr: Clean, Parse, Harmonize, Match, and Geocode Messy Real-World US Addresses},   author = {Cole Brokamp and Erika Manning},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.99.7},   url = {https://github.com/geomarker-io/addr}, }

what: Clean, Parse, Harmonize, Match, and Geocode Messy Real-World US Addresses
title: Clean, Parse, Harmonize, Match, and Geocode Messy Real-World US Addresses
text: Addresses validated time collection often heterogenously formatted filled typographical phonetic noise, making difficult compare link sets addresses. goal addr clean, parse, standardize, match messy, real-world US addresses R use data linkages. addr uses included usaddress library tag address components build vctrs-based addr vectors, including addr() vector addr_number(), addr_street(), addr_place() subclass vectors. Addr addr_part vectors can standardized matched/joined using exact, best, fuzzy linkages. Ultimately, facilitates using addr vectors column data frame allows powerful computing nested address structures using standard R tools.

title: Clean, Parse, Harmonize, Match, and Geocode Messy Real-World US Addresses
text: Install latest stable release addr R-universe : , install development version addr GitHub : Installing addr GitHub requires working Rust toolchain; install one using rustup.
code: install.packages("addr", repos = c("https://geomarker-io.r-universe.dev", "https://cloud.r-project.org")) # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("cole-brokamp/addr")

title: Clean, Parse, Harmonize, Match, and Geocode Messy Real-World US Addresses
text: addr vectors behave like standard R vectors: recycle, subset, combine vctrs tooling. can parse text addr vector as_addr() build one subclass vectors addr().
code: addr_vec <- as_addr(c("3333 Burnet Ave Cincinnati OH 45229",                       "5130 Rapid Run Rd Cincinnati OH 45238"))

title: addr classes — addr_number
text: structures addr() addr_ classes derived subset United States Thoroughfare, Landmark, Postal Address Data Standard relevant residential, numbered thoroughfare addresses:   addr() combines addr_number(), addr_street(), addr_place() single addr vector:
code: Address    ├─ AddressNumber    │  ├─ AddressNumberPrefix    │  ├─ AddressNumber    │  ├─ AddressNumberSuffix    ├─ StreetName    │  ├─ StreetNamePreModifier    │  ├─ StreetNamePreDirectional    │  ├─ StreetNamePreType    │  ├─ StreetName    │  ├─ StreetNamePostType    │  └─ StreetNamePostDirectional    └─ Place       ├─ PlaceName       ├─ StateName       └─ ZipCode <addr>  @ number: <addr_number>  .. @ prefix  .. @ digits  .. @ suffix  @ street: <addr_street>  .. @ predirectional  .. @ premodifier  .. @ pretype  .. @ name  .. @ posttype  .. @ postdirectional  @ place : <addr_place>  .. @ name  .. @ state  .. @ zipcode

title: addr classes — addr_number
code: addr_number(   prefix = NA_character_,   digits = NA_character_,   suffix = NA_character_ )  addr_street(   predirectional = NA_character_,   premodifier = NA_character_,   pretype = NA_character_,   name = NA_character_,   posttype = NA_character_,   postdirectional = NA_character_,   map_posttype = TRUE,   map_directional = TRUE,   map_pretype = TRUE,   map_ordinal = TRUE )  addr_place(   name = NA_character_,   state = NA_character_,   zipcode = NA_character_,   map_state = TRUE )  addr(number = addr_number(), street = addr_street(), place = addr_place())

title: addr classes — addr_number
text: prefix (often fractional) appears digits digits primary street number address suffix (often letter/part) attached digits predirectional direction name premodifier descriptive modifier name pretype type/classification name name street name city/town/municipality name posttype type/classification name postdirectional direction name map_posttype logical; map posttype abbreviations? map_directional logical; map pre- post-directional abbreviations? map_pretype logical; map pretype abbreviations? map_ordinal logical; map ordinal street names abbreviations? state state territory abbreviation zipcode ZIP code (must five digits starting "000") map_state logical; map state abbreviations? number addr_number vector street addr_street vector place addr_place vector

title: addr classes — addr_number
text: addr, addr_number, addr_street, addr_place vector

title: addr classes — addr_number
text: field values must must character vector least length one (including missing values); length one fields recycled match length fields.

title: addr classes — addr_number
code: # define a new addr_number vector addr_number(digits = "290") #> <addr_number> function ()   #>  @ prefix: chr NA #>  @ digits: chr "290" #>  @ suffix: chr NA addr_number(prefix = "N", digits = "290", suffix = "A") #> <addr_number> function ()   #>  @ prefix: chr "N" #>  @ digits: chr "290" #>  @ suffix: chr "A"  # define a new addr_street vector addr_street(name = "Burnet", posttype = "Ave") #> <addr_street> function ()   #>  @ predirectional : chr NA #>  @ premodifier    : chr NA #>  @ pretype        : chr NA #>  @ name           : chr "Burnet" #>  @ posttype       : chr "Ave" #>  @ postdirectional: chr NA  # street names are automatically mapped to abbreviations addr_street(predirectional = "North", name = "Fifth", posttype = "Street") #> <addr_street> function ()   #>  @ predirectional : chr "N" #>  @ premodifier    : chr NA #>  @ pretype        : chr NA #>  @ name           : chr "5TH" #>  @ posttype       : chr "St" #>  @ postdirectional: chr NA  # define a new addr_place vector addr_place(name = "Cincinnati", state = "OH", zipcode = "45220") #> <addr_place> function ()   #>  @ name   : chr "Cincinnati" #>  @ state  : chr "OH" #>  @ zipcode: chr "45220"  # define a new addr vector addr(   addr_number(digits = "290"),   addr_street(name = "Burnet", posttype = "Ave"),   addr_place(name = "Cincinnati", state = "OH", zipcode = "45229") ) #> <addr> #>  @ number: <addr_number> function ()   #>  .. @ prefix: chr NA #>  .. @ digits: chr "290" #>  .. @ suffix: chr NA #>  @ street: <addr_street> function ()   #>  .. @ predirectional : chr NA #>  .. @ premodifier    : chr NA #>  .. @ pretype        : chr NA #>  .. @ name           : chr "Burnet" #>  .. @ posttype       : chr "Ave" #>  .. @ postdirectional: chr NA #>  @ place : <addr_place> function ()   #>  .. @ name   : chr "Cincinnati" #>  .. @ state  : chr "OH" #>  .. @ zipcode: chr "45229"  # define a more complicated addr vector # and explicltly specify empty components to avoid NA addr(   addr_number(prefix = "", digits = "200", suffix = ""),   addr_street(     predirectional = "west",     premodifier = "Old",     pretype = "US",     name = "50",     posttype = "avenue",     postdirectional = "east",     map_directional = TRUE,     map_pretype = TRUE,     map_posttype = TRUE   ),   addr_place(name = "Cincinnati", state = "ohio", zipcode = "45220") ) #> <addr> #>  @ number: <addr_number> function ()   #>  .. @ prefix: chr "" #>  .. @ digits: chr "200" #>  .. @ suffix: chr "" #>  @ street: <addr_street> function ()   #>  .. @ predirectional : chr "W" #>  .. @ premodifier    : chr "Old" #>  .. @ pretype        : chr "US Hwy" #>  .. @ name           : chr "50" #>  .. @ posttype       : chr "Ave" #>  .. @ postdirectional: chr "E" #>  @ place : <addr_place> function ()   #>  .. @ name   : chr "Cincinnati" #>  .. @ state  : chr "OH" #>  .. @ zipcode: chr "45220"  # addr_* vectors are recycled and omitted fields are missing addr(   addr_number(digits = c("290", "200", "3333", "111")),   addr_street(     name = c("Burnet", "Main", "Ludlow", "State Route 32"),     posttype = c("Ave", "St", "Ave", NA_character_)   ),   addr_place(name = "Cincinnati", state = "OH") ) #> <addr> #>  @ number: <addr_number> function ()   #>  .. @ prefix: chr [1:4] NA NA NA NA #>  .. @ digits: chr [1:4] "290" "200" "3333" "111" #>  .. @ suffix: chr [1:4] NA NA NA NA #>  @ street: <addr_street> function ()   #>  .. @ predirectional : chr [1:4] NA NA NA NA #>  .. @ premodifier    : chr [1:4] NA NA NA NA #>  .. @ pretype        : chr [1:4] NA NA NA NA #>  .. @ name           : chr [1:4] "Burnet" "Main" "Ludlow" "State Route 32" #>  .. @ posttype       : chr [1:4] "Ave" "St" "Ave" NA #>  .. @ postdirectional: chr [1:4] NA NA NA NA #>  @ place : <addr_place> function ()   #>  .. @ name   : chr [1:4] "Cincinnati" "Cincinnati" "Cincinnati" "Cincinnati" #>  .. @ state  : chr [1:4] "OH" "OH" "OH" "OH" #>  .. @ zipcode: chr [1:4] NA NA NA NA

what: Left join two data frames using fuzzy addr matching — addr_fuzzy_left_join
title: Left join two data frames using fuzzy addr matching — addr_fuzzy_left_join
text: wraps around addr fuzzy matching helpers returns left-join style result. addr columns matched index rows expanded one--many many--many matches. See addr_match() addr_left_join() alternative strategy matching addr joining data frames addr columns faster, limited one best match instead multi-matches.

title: Left join two data frames using fuzzy addr matching — addr_fuzzy_left_join
code: addr_fuzzy_left_join(   x,   y,   by = "addr",   addr_fields = NULL,   suffix = c(".x", ".y"),   progress = interactive() )

title: Left join two data frames using fuzzy addr matching — addr_fuzzy_left_join
text: x, y data frames tibbles addr column addr column name x (y ); length-2 character vector c(x_col, y_col) addr_fields named vector osa_max_distances; max distances addr tag field present default used (see details). suffix character vector length 2 used suffix duplicate columns progress logical; show progress bar processing matched ZIP groups?

title: Left join two data frames using fuzzy addr matching — addr_fuzzy_left_join
text: data frame left-join semantics; note row order changed compared x

title: Left join two data frames using fuzzy addr matching — addr_fuzzy_left_join
text: addr_fuzzy_left_join works matching addresses grouped ZIP codes, specified osa_max_distances place fields ignored Defaults addr_fields: number_prefix: 0 number_digits: 0 number_suffix: 0 street_predirectional: 0 street_premodifier: 0 street_pretype: 0 street_name: 1 street_posttype: 0 street_postdirectional: 0

title: Left join two data frames using fuzzy addr matching — addr_fuzzy_left_join

what: Fuzzy match addr vectors using field-specific string distances — addr_fuzzy_match
title: Fuzzy match addr vectors using field-specific string distances — addr_fuzzy_match
text: addr_fuzzy_match() matches two addr vectors using one address tag fuzzy_match_addr_field() matches two addr vectors using single address tag Distances address tags defined using optimized string alignment; see fuzzy_match() stringdist::stringdist() details.

title: Fuzzy match addr vectors using field-specific string distances — addr_fuzzy_match
code: addr_fuzzy_match(x, y, addr_fields = NULL)  fuzzy_match_addr_field(   x,   y,   addr_field = c("number_prefix", "number_digits", "number_suffix",     "street_predirectional", "street_premodifier", "street_pretype", "street_name",     "street_posttype", "street_postdirectional", "place_name", "place_state",     "place_zipcode"),   osa_max_dist = 0 )

title: Fuzzy match addr vectors using field-specific string distances — addr_fuzzy_match
text: x addr vector match y addr vector match addr_fields named vector osa_max_distances; max distances addr tag field present default used (see details). addr_field character name single addr field match osa_max_dist maximum optimized string alignment distance used threshold matching single addr field

title: Fuzzy match addr vectors using field-specific string distances — addr_fuzzy_match
text: list integer vectors representing position best matching address(es) y address x

title: Fuzzy match addr vectors using field-specific string distances — addr_fuzzy_match
text: Defaults addr_fields: number_prefix: 0 number_digits: 0 number_suffix: 0 street_predirectional: 0 street_premodifier: 0 street_pretype: 0 street_name: 1 street_posttype: 0 street_postdirectional: 0 place_name: 0 place_state: 0 place_zipcode: 0 fuzzy matching street_name, "phonetic_street_key" prefilter automatically used (see ?fuzzy_match).

title: Fuzzy match addr vectors using field-specific string distances — addr_fuzzy_match
code: x_addr <- as_addr(c("123 Main St.", "333 Burnet Ave", "3333 Foofy Ave")) y_addr <- as_addr(c("0000 Main Street", "3333 Burnet Avenue", "222 Burnet Ave"))  # no matches with defaults addr_fuzzy_match(x_addr, y_addr) #> [[1]] #> integer(0) #>  #> [[2]] #> integer(0) #>  #> [[3]] #> integer(0) #>   # match on osa_max_dist of 2 for the address number addr_fuzzy_match(x_addr, y_addr, addr_fields = c("number_digits" = 2)) #> [[1]] #> integer(0) #>  #> [[2]] #> [1] 2 #>  #> [[3]] #> integer(0) #>   # ignore address number when matching addr_fuzzy_match(x_addr, y_addr, addr_fields = c("number_digits" = Inf)) #> [[1]] #> [1] 1 #>  #> [[2]] #> [1] 2 3 #>  #> [[3]] #> integer(0) #>    fuzzy_match_addr_field(   as_addr(c("123 Main St.", "3333 Burnet Ave", "3333 Foofy Ave")),   as_addr(c("0000 Main Street", "0000 Burnet Avenue", "222 Burnet Ave")),   addr_field = "street_name", osa_max_dist = 1 ) #> [[1]] #> [1] 1 #>  #> [[2]] #> [1] 2 3 #>  #> [[3]] #> integer(0) #>   # empty address fields have an OSA distance of zero and always match fuzzy_match_addr_field(   as_addr(c("123 Main St.", "3333 Burnet Ave", "3333 Foofy Ave")),   as_addr(c("0000 Main Street", "0000 Burnet Avenue", "222 Burnet Ave")),   addr_field = "number_prefix" ) #> [[1]] #> [1] 1 2 3 #>  #> [[2]] #> [1] 1 2 3 #>  #> [[3]] #> [1] 1 2 3 #>

what: Left join two data frames using addr matching — addr_left_join
title: Left join two data frames using addr matching — addr_left_join
text: addr_left_join() convenience wrapper around addr_match() returns left-join style result. expands rows x duplicate rows original y share exact matched addr, return multiple distinct candidate addresses y. addr_match() still selects single best address wrapper expands exact duplicates.

title: Left join two data frames using addr matching — addr_left_join
code: addr_left_join(   x,   y,   by = "addr",   suffix = c(".x", ".y"),   zip_variants = TRUE,   name_phonetic_dist = 2L,   name_fuzzy_dist = 1L,   number_fuzzy_dist = 1L,   match_street_predirectional = TRUE,   match_street_posttype = TRUE,   match_street_pretype = TRUE,   match_street_postdirectional = FALSE,   progress = interactive() )

title: Left join two data frames using addr matching — addr_left_join
text: x, y data frames tibbles addr column addr column name x (y ); length-2 character vector c(x_col, y_col) suffix character vector length 2 used suffix duplicate columns zip_variants logical; fuzzy match common variants x y? (e.g., changing 4th 5th digit) name_phonetic_dist integer; maximum optimized string alignment distance phonetic_street_key() x y consider possible match name_fuzzy_dist integer; maximum optimized string alignment distance @name x y consider possible match number_fuzzy_dist integer; maximum optimized string alignment distance @number x y consider possible match match_street_predirectional logical; require street predirectional match selecting street candidates? match_street_posttype logical; require street posttype match selecting street candidates? match_street_pretype logical; require street pretype match selecting street candidates? match_street_postdirectional logical; require street postdirectional match selecting street candidates? progress logical; show addr_match() progress?

title: Left join two data frames using addr matching — addr_left_join
text: data frame left-join semantics. Duplicate rows y exact matched addr returned. Partial ZIP-street-matches expand multiple candidate rows y.

title: Left join two data frames using addr matching — addr_left_join
code: the_addr <- nad("Hamilton", "OH", refresh = "no") my_addr <- tibble::tibble(   addr = as_addr(voter_addresses()[1:100]),   id = 1:100 ) addr_left_join(my_addr, the_addr, by = c("addr", "nad_addr")) #> # A tibble: 111 × 9 #>    addr            id nad_addr.y subaddress uuid  date_update s2    address_type #>    <addr>       <int> <addr>     <chr>      <chr> <date>      <s2_> <chr>        #>  1 3359 QUEEN …     1 3359 QUEE… NA         {E3A… 2025-03-30  -6.7… Unknown      #>  2 1040 KREIS …     2 1040 KREI… NA         {D05… 2025-03-30  -6.7… Unknown      #>  3 9960 DALY R…     3 9960 DALY… NA         {109… 2025-03-30  -6.1… Unknown      #>  4 413 VOLKERT…     4 413 VOLKE… NA         {1BB… 2025-03-30  -6.7… Unknown      #>  5 8519 LINDER…     5 8519 LIND… NA         {F78… 2025-03-30  -6.6… Unknown      #>  6 6361 BEECHM…     6 6361 BEEC… NA         {6D4… 2025-03-30  -6.6… Unknown      #>  7 10466 ADVEN…     7 10466 ADV… NA         {1D1… 2025-03-30  -6.1… Unknown      #>  8 3156 LOOKOU…     8 3156 LOOK… NA         {AE8… 2025-03-30  -6.6… Unknown      #>  9 310 WYOMING…     9 310 WYOMI… NA         {331… 2025-03-30  -6.1… Unknown      #> 10 118 SPRINGF…    10 118 SPRIN… NA         {F3E… 2025-03-30  -6.1… Unknown      #> # ℹ 101 more rows #> # ℹ 1 more variable: parcel_id <chr> # some addresses may match with more than one address in NAD # since matching does not consider subaddress (e.g. "line two")

title: Match addr vectors — addr_match
text: single addr y chosen addr x. Matching staged reduce search space: ZIP codes matched first, street names matched within matched ZIP code, street numbers finally matched within matched street ZIP code combination. one candidate addr remains y stages, first candidate y returned. Missing empty address components matched stage left missing returned addr() values. Rows matched ZIP code street match return addr @place@zipcode filled; rows matched ZIP code street number match also return matched @street. addr_match() accepts raw reference data prepares internally, right default one-matching jobs. addr_match_prepare() becomes useful reference y reused across multiple calls addr_match(), caches deduplicated reference addresses ZIP/street/number candidate lookups instead rebuilding every call. Preparing y avoids recomputing unique(y), ZIP-code groups, exact street/number candidate lookups time call addr_match() reference addresses. single end--end match, preparing y explicitly remove work; moves outside addr_match().

title: Match addr vectors — addr_match
code: addr_match(   x,   y,   zip_variants = TRUE,   name_phonetic_dist = 2L,   name_fuzzy_dist = 1L,   number_fuzzy_dist = 1L,   match_street_predirectional = TRUE,   match_street_posttype = TRUE,   match_street_pretype = TRUE,   match_street_postdirectional = FALSE,   progress = interactive() )  addr_match_prepare(y)

title: Match addr vectors — addr_match
text: x addr vector match y addr vector match , prepared addr_match_index created addr_match_prepare() zip_variants logical; fuzzy match common variants x y? (e.g., changing 4th 5th digit) name_phonetic_dist integer; maximum optimized string alignment distance phonetic_street_key() x y consider possible match name_fuzzy_dist integer; maximum optimized string alignment distance @name x y consider possible match number_fuzzy_dist integer; maximum optimized string alignment distance @number x y consider possible match match_street_predirectional logical; require street predirectional match selecting street candidates? match_street_posttype logical; require street posttype match selecting street candidates? match_street_pretype logical; require street pretype match selecting street candidates? match_street_postdirectional logical; require street postdirectional match selecting street candidates? progress logical; show reference-preparation timing raw y progress bar processing matched ZIP groups?

title: Match addr vectors — addr_match
text: addr vector, length x, best match y addr x. Partial matches returned matched ZIP code /street fields filled later stages match.

title: Classify addr match stage — addr_match_stage
text: Classify addr vector staged outcomes returned addr_match(): match, ZIP-match, ZIP-plus-street match, ZIP-plus-street-plus-number match.

title: Classify addr match stage — addr_match_stage
code: addr_match_stage(x, strict = TRUE)

title: Classify addr match stage — addr_match_stage
text: x addr vector classify strict logical; require x follow partial-result structure produced addr_match()? FALSE, classification based deepest non-missing core component (@place@zipcode, @street@name, @number@digits).

title: Classify addr match stage — addr_match_stage
text: ordered factor levels none, zip, street, number

title: Classify addr match stage — addr_match_stage
code: y <- as_addr(c(   "10 MAIN ST CINCINNATI OH 45220",   "11 MAIN ST CINCINNATI OH 45220",   "10 MAIN ST CINCINNATI OH 45229" )) x <- as_addr(c(   "99 MAIN ST CINCINNATI OH 45220",   "10 OAK ST CINCINNATI OH 45220",   "10 MAIN ST CINCINNATI OH 45103" ))  out <- addr_match(x, y) addr_match_stage(out) #> [1] street zip    none   #> Levels: none < zip < street < number

title: Coerce to addr — as_addr
text: character: cleaned (clean = TRUE) clean_address_text() tagged using usaddress_tag(); tags normalized abbreviations passing map_* arguments addr_street() addr_place(); ZIP codes parsed five characters truncated warning; non-numeric characters parsed address number digits removed warning data.frame: must columns named according fields addr_number(), addr_street(), addr_place(); also passes map_* arguments addr_street() addr_place() addr: returned -

title: Fuzzy match — fuzzy_match
text: Fuzzy match strings x y using optimized string alignment (ignoring capitalization).

title: Fuzzy match — fuzzy_match
code: fuzzy_match(x, y, osa_max_dist = 1, prefilter = c("none", "psk"))

title: Fuzzy match — fuzzy_match
text: x character vector match y character vector match osa_max_dist maximum OSA distance consider match; Inf special case avoids computing string distance returning y instead just best match(es) 'y` prefilter method used prefilter y computing osa distances fuzzy matching speed calculations; "none" nothing, "psk" removes addrs y phonetic_street_key() x

title: Fuzzy match — fuzzy_match
text: list integer vectors representing position best matching string(s) y string x

title: Fuzzy match — fuzzy_match
text: multiple strings y tied minimum osa distances string x, indices included return value.

title: Fuzzy match — fuzzy_match
code: my_names <-   c("Pinye", "Pine", "Oalck", "Sunset", "Riverbend", "Greenfild") the_names <-   c("Piney", "Pine", "Oak", "Cheshire", "Greenfield", "Maple", "Elm") matches <- fuzzy_match(my_names, the_names, osa_max_dist = 1) matches #> [[1]] #> [1] 1 2 #>  #> [[2]] #> [1] 2 #>  #> [[3]] #> integer(0) #>  #> [[4]] #> integer(0) #>  #> [[5]] #> integer(0) #>  #> [[6]] #> [1] 5 #>   lapply(matches, \(i) the_names[i]) #> [[1]] #> [1] "Piney" "Pine"  #>  #> [[2]] #> [1] "Pine" #>  #> [[3]] #> character(0) #>  #> [[4]] #> character(0) #>  #> [[5]] #> character(0) #>  #> [[6]] #> [1] "Greenfield" #>   x <- as_addr(voter_addresses()[1:100])@street@name y <- unique(nad_example_data()$nad_addr@street@name) system.time(fuzzy_match(x, y)) #>    user  system elapsed  #>   0.471   0.005   0.162  # larger vectors see a speedup when using # phonetic_street_key as a prefilter # but may miss potential matches that are within # osa_max_dist of each other, but did not have # identical phonetic codes (e.g., "woolper" and "woopler") system.time(fuzzy_match(x, y, prefilter = "psk")) #>    user  system elapsed  #>   0.282   0.003   0.132

what: Geocode using TIGER address features — geocode_tiger
title: Geocode using TIGER address features — geocode_tiger
text: geocode_tiger() geocodes addr vectors using TIGER address range geometries downloaded census.gov specific county. geocoding precision level matching achieved address: range: precise; address matched street name ZIP code, number within one ranges; point locations interpolated geometry best match using street number street: less precise; address matched street name ZIP code, number within one ranges; point locations interpolated centroid matching range geometries unmatched: precision; address match street name ZIP code combinations; point locations <null feature> (equivalent NA)

title: Geocode using TIGER address features — geocode_tiger
code: geocode_tiger(x, county, year, offset = 0)

title: Geocode using TIGER address features — geocode_tiger
text: x addr vector county character string county identifier year character year tigris product offset number meters offset geocode street line

title: Geocode using TIGER address features — geocode_tiger
text: s2_geography vector geocoded point locations; attribute ("geocode_precision") added factor levels range, street, unmatched.

title: Geocode using TIGER address features — geocode_tiger
text: street number-name multiple range parity matches, best match chosen based smallest width ranges range closest midpoint.

title: Geocode using TIGER address features — geocode_tiger
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ gcd <-   geocode_tiger(as_addr(voter_addresses()[1:100]),                 county = "39061", year = "2024", offset = 20)  head(gcd)  table(attr(gcd, "geocode_precision"))  # consider addresses matched to street but out of range missing gcd[attr(gcd, "geocode_precision") == "street"] <- NA_character_  # convert to s2_cell s2::as_s2_cell(gcd)  # create leaflet map leaflet::leaflet(wk::wk_coords(gcd)) |>   leaflet::addTiles() |>   leaflet::addCircleMarkers( lng = ~x, lat = ~y, label = ~ feature_id) } # }

title: Match addr_number vectors — match_addr_number
text: single addr_number y chosen addr_number x. exact matches (using .character) found, possible matches (within number_fuzzy_distance) searched y. multiple matches present y, best one selected based lowest absolute numeric difference @digits x; ties broken optimized string alignment (OSA) distances preferring lowest value sorted Lexicographic order digits preceding alphabetic characters. addr_number objects missing @digits empty strings @prefix, @digits, @suffix matched returned missing instead.

title: Match addr_number vectors — match_addr_number
code: match_addr_number(x, y, number_fuzzy_dist = 1L)

title: Match addr_number vectors — match_addr_number
text: x, y addr_number vectors match number_fuzzy_dist integer; maximum optimized string alignment distance @number x y consider possible match

title: Match addr_number vectors — match_addr_number
text: addr_number vector, length x, best match y addr_number code x; best match found missing value returned (addr_number())

title: Match addr_number vectors — match_addr_number
code: x <- addr_number(    prefix = "",    digits = as.character(c(1, 10, 228, 11, 22, 22, 22, 10, 99897, NA)),    suffix = ""  )  y <- addr_number(   prefix = "",   digits = as.character(c(12, 11, 10, 22)),   suffix = "" )  match_addr_number(x, y) #> <addr_number> function ()   #>  @ prefix: chr [1:10] "" "" "" "" "" "" "" "" NA NA #>  @ digits: chr [1:10] "10" "10" "22" "11" "22" "22" "22" "10" NA NA #>  @ suffix: chr [1:10] "" "" "" "" "" "" "" "" NA NA  match_addr_number(x, y, number_fuzzy_dist = 0L) #> <addr_number> function ()   #>  @ prefix: chr [1:10] NA "" NA "" "" "" "" "" NA NA #>  @ digits: chr [1:10] NA "10" NA "11" "22" "22" "22" "10" NA NA #>  @ suffix: chr [1:10] NA "" NA "" "" "" "" "" NA NA

title: Match addr_street vectors — match_addr_street
text: single addr_street y chosen addr_street x. exact matches (using .character) found, possible matches chosen fuzzy matching street name (using phonetic street key street name) exact matching enabled street components. Ordinal street names use restricted phonetic candidates: ordinal phonetic key like #0007 may fuzzy match plausible ordinal neighbors digit shifts (#0070, #0700, #7000) -width substitutions (#0008, #0009), arbitrary OSA-distance-one ordinal keys #0017 #0077. Ties broken .......... first now. addr_street objects within missing empty @name matched returned missing instead.

title: Match addr_street vectors — match_addr_street
code: match_addr_street(   x,   y,   name_phonetic_dist = 1L,   name_fuzzy_dist = 2L,   match_street_predirectional = TRUE,   match_street_posttype = TRUE,   match_street_pretype = TRUE,   match_street_postdirectional = FALSE )

title: Match addr_street vectors — match_addr_street
text: x, y addr_street vectors match name_phonetic_dist integer; maximum optimized string alignment distance phonetic_street_key() x y consider possible match name_fuzzy_dist integer; maximum optimized string alignment distance @name x y consider possible match match_street_predirectional logical; require street predirectional match selecting street candidates? match_street_posttype logical; require street posttype match selecting street candidates? match_street_pretype logical; require street pretype match selecting street candidates? match_street_postdirectional logical; require street postdirectional match selecting street candidates?

title: Match addr_street vectors — match_addr_street
text: addr_street vector, length x, best match y addr_street code x; best match found missing value returned (addr_street())

title: Match addr_street vectors — match_addr_street
code: my_streets <- addr_street(    predirectional = "",    premodifier = "",    pretype = "",    name = c("Beechview", "Vivian", "Springfield", "Round Bottom", "Pfeiffer", "Beachview",             "Vevan", "Srpingfield", "Square Top", "Pfeffer", "Wuhlper", ""),   posttype = c("Cir", "Pl", "Pike", "Rd", "Rd", "Cir", "Pl", "Pike", "Rd", "Rd", "Ave", ""),   postdirectional = ""  ) the_streets <- nad_example_data()$nad_addr@street match_addr_street(my_streets, the_streets) #> <addr_street> function ()   #>  @ predirectional : chr [1:12] "" "" "" "" "" "" "" "" NA "" "" NA #>  @ premodifier    : chr [1:12] "" "" "" "" "" "" "" "" NA "" "" NA #>  @ pretype        : chr [1:12] "" "" "" "" "" "" "" "" NA "" "" NA #>  @ name           : chr [1:12] "BEECHVIEW" "VIVIAN" "SPRINGFIELD" "ROUND BOTTOM" ... #>  @ posttype       : chr [1:12] "Cir" "Pl" "Pike" "Rd" "Rd" "Cir" "Pl" "Pike" NA "Rd" ... #>  @ postdirectional: chr [1:12] "" "" "" "" "" "" "" "" NA "" "" NA  toggle_y <- addr_street(   predirectional = c("E", "", "", "E"),   premodifier = "",   pretype = c("", "", "US Hwy", "US Hwy"),   name = c("14th", "Oak", "Main", "Main"),   posttype = c("St", "Rd", "Rd", "Rd"),   postdirectional = c("", "", "", "E"),   map_pretype = FALSE,   map_posttype = FALSE,   map_directional = FALSE,   map_ordinal = FALSE )  # predirectional is required by default, so blank "14th St" stays unmatched format(match_addr_street(   addr_street(     predirectional = "",     premodifier = "",     pretype = "",     name = "14th",     posttype = "St",     postdirectional = "",     map_pretype = FALSE,     map_posttype = FALSE,     map_directional = FALSE,     map_ordinal = FALSE   ),   toggle_y )) #> [1] "" format(match_addr_street(   addr_street(     predirectional = "",     premodifier = "",     pretype = "",     name = "14th",     posttype = "St",     postdirectional = "",     map_pretype = FALSE,     map_posttype = FALSE,     map_directional = FALSE,     map_ordinal = FALSE   ),   toggle_y,   match_street_predirectional = FALSE )) #> [1] "E 14th St"  # posttype can also be made optional during fuzzy street-name matching format(match_addr_street(   addr_street(     predirectional = "",     premodifier = "",     pretype = "",     name = "Oka",     posttype = "Ave",     postdirectional = "",     map_pretype = FALSE,     map_posttype = FALSE,     map_directional = FALSE,     map_ordinal = FALSE   ),   toggle_y,   name_fuzzy_dist = 1L )) #> [1] "" format(match_addr_street(   addr_street(     predirectional = "",     premodifier = "",     pretype = "",     name = "Oka",     posttype = "Ave",     postdirectional = "",     map_pretype = FALSE,     map_posttype = FALSE,     map_directional = FALSE,     map_ordinal = FALSE   ),   toggle_y,   name_fuzzy_dist = 1L,   match_street_posttype = FALSE )) #> [1] "Oak Rd"  # pretype is required by default; postdirectional can also be required format(match_addr_street(   addr_street(     predirectional = "E",     premodifier = "",     pretype = "US Hwy",     name = "Mian",     posttype = "Rd",     postdirectional = "E",     map_pretype = FALSE,     map_posttype = FALSE,     map_directional = FALSE,     map_ordinal = FALSE   ),   toggle_y,   match_street_pretype = FALSE,   name_fuzzy_dist = 1L )) #> [1] "E US Hwy Main Rd E" format(match_addr_street(   addr_street(     predirectional = "E",     premodifier = "",     pretype = "US Hwy",     name = "Mian",     posttype = "Rd",     postdirectional = "E",     map_pretype = FALSE,     map_posttype = FALSE,     map_directional = FALSE,     map_ordinal = FALSE   ),   toggle_y,   name_fuzzy_dist = 1L )) #> [1] "E US Hwy Main Rd E" format(match_addr_street(   addr_street(     predirectional = "E",     premodifier = "",     pretype = "US Hwy",     name = "Mian",     posttype = "Rd",     postdirectional = "E",     map_pretype = FALSE,     map_posttype = FALSE,     map_directional = FALSE,     map_ordinal = FALSE   ),   toggle_y,   name_fuzzy_dist = 1L,   match_street_pretype = TRUE,   match_street_postdirectional = TRUE )) #> [1] "E US Hwy Main Rd E"

title: Match ZIP codes — match_zipcodes
text: single ZIP code y chosen ZIP code x. default, exact matches found, common variants ZIP codes x searched y. multiple variants present y, best one selected based lowest absolute numeric difference ZIP code x; ties broken OSA string distances preferring minimum number.

what: Read National Address Database (NAD) tables into R — nad
title: Read National Address Database (NAD) tables into R — nad
text: U.S. Department Transportation partners address programs state, local, tribal governments compile authoritative data database. Find information : https://www.transportation.gov/gis/national-address-database nad_read() reads data NAD geodatabase county state, downloading first R user's data directory addr package already downloaded nad_download(), readies R. NAD geodatabase large size disk (~10 GB). Data binaries cached outputs nad_read() County/State created first run nad(). Download data binaries R_user_dir cache folder point R (?R_user_dir) files disk read NAD tables without download nationwide NAD geodatabase. (list.files(tools::R_user_dir("addr", "cache"), recursive = TRUE))

title: Read National Address Database (NAD) tables into R — nad
code: nad(county, state, refresh = c("yes", "no", "force"))  nad_read(county, state)  nad_download()

title: Read National Address Database (NAD) tables into R — nad
text: county character, length one; name county state character, length one; name state refresh character, length one; choose refresh NAD data binaries cached disk already present; "yes" create data binary already present, "" error data binary already present, "force" create data binary overwrite existing data binary

title: Read National Address Database (NAD) tables into R — nad
text: NAD downloaded latest release transportation.gov data portal: https://data.transportation.gov/dataset/National-Address-Database-NAD-File-Geodatabase/yw36-suxr/about_data original schema, see https://www.transportation.gov/sites/dot.gov/files/2023-07/NAD_Schema_202304.pdf NAD distinguish empty missing address components. reading R, missing address components replaced empty string ("") except address number (digits), street name, ZIP code.

title: Read National Address Database (NAD) tables into R — nad

what: Convert street names into phonetic matching keys — phonetic_street_key
title: Convert street names into phonetic matching keys — phonetic_street_key
text: Ordinal street names (e.g., "11TH", "5TH") encoded zero-padded numeric identifiers special prefix, non-ordinal street names encoded using Soundex phonetic code (see ?stringdist::phonetic). Ordinal words (e.g., "Eleventh", "Fifth") detected converted automatically. phonetic key exactly four characters long.

title: Convert street names into phonetic matching keys — phonetic_street_key
code: phonetic_street_key(x)

text: x character vector

title: Tag US addresses — tag_usaddress
text: Addresses tagged using usaddress conditional random field rust port usaddress. Possible address labels include: AddressNumberPrefix AddressNumberSuffix AddressNumber BuildingName CornerOf IntersectionSeparator LandmarkName NotAddress OccupancyIdentifier OccupancyType PlaceName Recipient StateName StreetNamePostDirectional StreetNamePostType StreetNamePreDirectional StreetNamePreModifier StreetNamePreType StreetName SubaddressIdentifier SubaddressType USPSBoxGroupID USPSBoxGroupType USPSBoxID USPSBoxType ZipCode Find information definitions https://www.fgdc.gov/standards/projects/address-data

what: Get s2_geography for tiger street ranges — tiger_addr_feat
title: Get s2_geography for tiger street ranges — tiger_addr_feat
text: TIGER address features (.e. street address ranges) read compressed addrfeat shapefiles county Census vintage. already present, compressed addrfeat shapefiles downloaded census.gov FTP site R user's data directory addr package. reading R, data converted "long" format street side L/R used tiger_geocode().

title: Get s2_geography for tiger street ranges — tiger_addr_feat
code: tiger_addr_feat(county, year)

title: Get s2_geography for tiger street ranges — tiger_addr_feat
text: county character string county identifier year character year tigris product

text: tibble LINEARID, FULLNAME, side, ZIP, FROMHN, TOHN, PARITY, OFFSET, s2_geography columns

what: Run usaddress::parse in Rust on a character vector in R — usaddress_tag
title: Run usaddress::parse in Rust on a character vector in R — usaddress_tag
text: Use tag_usaddress() instead function directly.

title: Run usaddress::parse in Rust on a character vector in R — usaddress_tag
code: usaddress_tag(x)

title: Example addresses — voter_addresses
text: voter_addresses data generated example character vector real-world addresses. addresses downloaded Hamilton County, Ohio voter registration database 2024-09-12. See inst/make_example_addresses.R details. AddressPreDirectional, AddressNumber, AddressStreet, AddressSuffix, CityName, "OH", AddressZip pasted together create 242,133 unique addresses registered voters Hamilton County, OH.

title: Example addresses — voter_addresses
code: voter_addresses()

title: Example addresses — voter_addresses
text: character vector

title: Example addresses — voter_addresses
code: voter_addresses() |>   head() #> [1] "3359 QUEEN CITY AVE CINCINNATI OH 45238" #> [2] "1040 KREIS LN CINCINNATI OH 45205"       #> [3] "9960 DALY RD CINCINNATI OH 45231"        #> [4] "413 VOLKERT PL CINCINNATI OH 45219"      #> [5] "8519 LINDERWOOD LN CINCINNATI OH 45255"  #> [6] "6361 BEECHMONT AVE CINCINNATI OH 45230"
